input.background: Suppose an experiment of a new product can result in exactly m possible outcomes and their corresponding probabilities are s1, …, sm and p1, …, pm, respectively. Suppose the experiment is, independently, repeated n times and let Xi be the number of outcomes of type si for i = 1, 2, …, m.
input.question: What is the probability distribution of the outcomes when the experiment is repeated n times independently?

output.answer: It has the multinomial distribution as follows: $$\begin{aligned} &P\left[X_1=s_1, X_2=s_2, \ldots, X_k=s_m\right]=\left\{\begin{array}{l} \frac{n!}{s_{1}!s_{2}!\ldots s_{m}!} p_1^{s_1} p_2^{s_2} \ldots p_m^{s_k} \\ 0 \quad \ \text { otherwise } \end{array}\right.\\ &\ \text { where } s_i=0,1, \ldots, n \ \text { and } \sum_{i=1}^m s_i=n . \end{aligned}$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
